author: Staff Reporter
headline: SWIFT Tests Multi-CBDC Platform With 15 Central Banks
summary: SWIFT announced a pilot program connecting 15 central banks for multi-currency CBDC settlement, targeting a 2027 production launch. The system uses ISO 20022 messaging and atomic settlement across different CBDC platforms.
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - competes with Ripple CBDC platform

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Circle Receives Singapore MAS License for USDC Operations
summary: Circle Internet Financial has received a Major Payment Institution license from Singapore's Monetary Authority, enabling direct USDC issuance and redemption in the city-state. Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire called it 'a milestone for regulated stablecoin infrastructure in Asia.'
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - Circle expanding in Ripple's strongest region

author: Staff Reporter
headline: XRP ETF Inflows Hit 40 Consecutive Days as Institutional Demand Holds
summary: Spot XRP ETFs recorded their 40th consecutive day of net inflows, adding $28M yesterday. Total cumulative inflows now stand at $1.52B across all products. Canary Capital's XRPC leads with 42% market share.

author: Staff Reporter
headline: JPMorgan Kinexys Onboards First Non-JPM Banks in Asia Expansion
summary: JPMorgan's Kinexys blockchain platform has onboarded 12 regional banks in Asia-Pacific, marking the first time the platform has been available outside JPMorgan's own client base. Banks in Japan, Singapore, and Australia are among the first wave.
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - Kinexys opening up in Ripple's key APAC market

author: Staff Reporter
headline: EU MiCA Framework Drives 40% Increase in Crypto License Applications
summary: European crypto companies filed 40% more regulatory applications in Q1 2026 compared to Q4 2025, driven by the Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation providing clearer operating frameworks. The largest categories were payment services and custody.
relevance_to_ripple: Indirect - positive for Ripple's EU expansion via Luxembourg

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Stablecoin Market Hits $250 Billion as Enterprise Adoption Accelerates
summary: The total stablecoin market capitalization crossed $250 billion for the first time, with Tether ($140B), USDC ($75B), and RLUSD ($1.5B) leading. Enterprise use cases including supply chain payments and treasury management are driving growth beyond crypto trading.
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - RLUSD mentioned as top 3 regulated stablecoin

author: @ResearchPulse
headline: THREAD: Analyzing XRP on-chain metrics post-correction
summary: On-chain data shows XRP active addresses holding steady at 1.2M daily despite 62% price drop from ATH. Settlement volume actually increased 15% MoM. This divergence between price and utility metrics is notable.

author: Staff Reporter
headline: US Treasury Secretary Calls for 'Comprehensive Digital Asset Framework' at G7
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent urged G7 finance ministers to coordinate on digital asset regulation, citing the GENIUS Act as a model. He specifically mentioned cross-border payment efficiency and stablecoin standards as priorities.
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - favorable regulatory direction for Ripple's business model

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Binance Launches Institutional Custody Service to Rival Coinbase and BitGo
summary: Binance announced an institutional-grade custody service with $100M insurance coverage, targeting hedge funds and asset managers. The service uses multi-party computation and supports 50+ assets.
relevance_to_ripple: Indirect - custody competition but different segment than Ripple

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Mastercard Expands Stablecoin Settlement Beyond RLUSD Pilot to Include USDC
summary: Mastercard is expanding its stablecoin settlement capabilities beyond its Ripple/RLUSD pilot to include Circle's USDC, enabling merchants to receive settlement in either stablecoin. The company says it's 'stablecoin agnostic' and focused on what works for clients.
relevance_to_ripple: Direct - Mastercard hedging bets; RLUSD not exclusive

author: @CryptoKaleo
headline: $DOGE looking ready for a breakout. Cup and handle forming on the daily. $1 target by summer.
summary: Popular crypto trader posts bullish Dogecoin analysis with technical chart patterns.

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Bank of England Publishes Digital Pound Architecture Paper
summary: The Bank of England released a detailed technical architecture for a potential digital pound (CBDC), describing a two-tier model where the central bank provides core ledger and private sector builds user-facing wallets. Consultation runs through June 2026.
relevance_to_ripple: Indirect - UK CBDC opportunity for Ripple's platform

author: Staff
headline: Bored Ape Floor Price Hits New Low as NFT Market Struggles
summary: Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs hit a floor price of 8 ETH, down from an all-time high of 128 ETH. The broader NFT market has contracted 85% from its 2022 peak.

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Hong Kong Grants First Stablecoin Licenses Under New Framework
summary: Hong Kong's Monetary Authority granted its first two stablecoin issuer licenses under the new regulatory framework to a local consortium and an international fintech firm. Analysts say this positions HK as Asia's regulated stablecoin hub.
relevance_to_ripple: Indirect - potential RLUSD expansion opportunity in HK

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Visa B2B Connect Adds 8 New Latin American Corridors
summary: Visa expanded its blockchain-based B2B settlement network to 8 new Latin American corridors, bringing the total to 38 globally. Settlement times remain at 24-48 hours but Visa says improvements are planned for 2026.
relevance_to_ripple: Moderate - Visa expanding in LATAM where Ripple has fewer partners

author: Staff Reporter
headline: Crypto Market Update: BTC $92K, ETH $3.8K, XRP $1.37 as Markets Consolidate
summary: Major cryptocurrencies traded in tight ranges as markets digested recent regulatory developments. Total crypto market cap: $3.2 trillion. XRP ETF inflows continue to provide support.
